text.title: Accessibility-First Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the accessibility-first-design methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Every text/background token pair MUST carry a `contrast_ratio` field; pairs failing 4.5:1 (body) or 3:1 (large/non-text) MUST be excluded from the system.
rule.rationale: Token-encoded contrast prevents brand colour from shipping below thresholds; the constraint lives where the design lives.

rule.statement: Components MUST be defined by semantic archetype (button, link, dialog, etc.) before visual styling; styling MUST NOT override role.
rule.rationale: Visual-first design routinely ships clickable divs and links-styled-as-buttons; archetype-first prevents the regression at source.

rule.statement: `:focus-visible` MUST be defined on every interactive token; `outline:none` without a replacement is forbidden across the system.
rule.rationale: Global outline:none with no replacement is the most common SC 2.4.7 violation.

rule.statement: Every motion token MUST have a `prefers-reduced-motion` variant declared; raw motion-only definitions are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Vestibular safety + SC 2.3.3; baking the variant into the token prevents per-component oversight.

rule.statement: Spacing/positioning tokens MUST be expressed as logical properties (inline-start/end) when the system targets RTL locales.
rule.rationale: left/right tokens break under dir=rtl; logical properties prevent RTL regressions.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
